description: Small curated Polymarket mirror seed set for the AI/technology devnet. Addressed by Polymarket Gamma event id so selection is deterministic and auditable. When `--curated-markets-path`/`CURATED_MARKETS_PATH` points at this file, the mirror fetches exactly these events by id and mirrors their active, non-closed child markets instead of scanning volume-ranked categories. The set deliberately excludes sports, weather, short-expiry markets, broad outcome ladders, and valuation grids. These 4 event entries expanded to 7 active child markets when verified on 2026-07-14. Each Sybil market retains its Polymarket condition id, parent event id, and event URL as provenance. To update: verify `https://gamma-api.polymarket.com/events?id=<event_id>` reports active=true, closed=false, and at least one active non-closed child, then add or remove an entry and redeploy sybil-polymarket.

events.title: Will a Chinese company have a top AI model by December 31?
events.note: Four durable benchmark-rank binaries (top 3, top 5, top 10, and best), all ending 2026-12-31. Closely matches Sybil's native AI-leaderboard theme without a large outcome fan-out.

events.title: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
AI bubble burst by...? (2026 leg)
events.note: One active 2026 binary. The March-2026 and December-2025 legs are closed and filtered out by the mirror. Durable macro/AI question ending 2026-12-31.

events.title: Will AI be charged with a crime before 2027?
